member.name: F:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ActorType.User
member.summary: When a human user initiates the action or when it is the result of an event propagated from a user action.

member.name: F:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ActorType.System
member.summary: When the system initiates something on its own. Examples: Nightly batch job, initial seed, timeout/scheduler, automatic compensation.

member.name: F:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ActorType.External
member.summary: When the intention arises outside your platform, outside your organizational control. ⚠️ Warning. Another microservice of yours is NOT external, other bounded contexts of the same system are NOT external.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ExecutorType
member.summary: Represents the system that is actually running the action.

member.name: F:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ExecutorType.Service
member.summary: A service, such as an API that is executing the action as part of its normal operation.

member.name: F:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ExecutorType.Worker
member.summary: A background worker or a scheduled job that is executing the action outside of the context of an API request, such as processing a queue or running a periodic task.

member.name: F:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ExecutorType.Tool
member.summary: A tool, such as a command-line interface (CLI) or an administrative dashboard, that is executing the action as part of a manual operation by an administrator or developer.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IAuthorizationPolicy
member.summary: A single named authorization policy. Implementations live in the application layer because they typically need to load a resource (via a repository) to evaluate ownership or other resource-bound conditions — this is application orchestration over domain/read model data, not a domain invariant.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IBaseCommand
member.summary: Marker interface to allow generic type constraints of objects implementing or .

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IBaseEventHandler`1
member.summary: Defines a common contract for handling events, regardless of the underlying event bus implementation (in-memory or external).
member.typeparam: The type of event to handle, which must implement .

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ICacheRequest
member.summary: ⚠️ Warning:. DO NOT USE WHEN USING EVENT SOURCING, AS IT CAN CAUSE INCONSISTENCIES. This interface is intended to be implemented by requests that require caching when processed, but not when using projections in an event bus pipeline.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ICommand`1
member.summary: Transactional Command with response operation. To see pure DDD commands check .

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ICommand
member.summary: Pure DDD transactional command. Commands will not use response operation check impure alternative .

member.name: M:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IExecutionContextService.SetExecutionContext(JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ExecutionContext)
member.summary: Sets the context for the first time. Throws if one already exists. Use in HTTP middleware, where a double assignment is always an error.

member.name: M:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IExecutionContextService.OverrideExecutionContext(JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ExecutionContext)
member.summary: Overrides the existing context. Use in workers and message consumers, where each message has its own independent context.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IInvalidateCacheRequest
member.summary: ⚠️ Warning:. DO NOT USE WHEN USING EVENT SOURCING, AS IT CAN CAUSE INCONSISTENCIES. This interface is intended to be implemented by requests that require cache invalidation when processed, but not when using projections in an event bus pipeline.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IPolicyEnforcer
member.summary: Evaluates named authorization policies that go beyond static role/permission checks. Policies receive only the current actor's identity/roles and an optional resourceId — never the raw request — keeping the enforcer decoupled from command/query shapes.
Evaluates a named policy against the current actor's identity/roles and an optional resourceId.
member.param: The authenticated user's id, from ExecutionContext.
The current user's roles within the active scope.
The current user's permissions within the active scope.
The id of the resource the policy evaluates ownership against (e.g. a ReservationId). Null for policies that don't need one.
member.returns: A indicating whether the policy was satisfied.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IPolicyResourceRequest
member.summary: Implemented by commands/queries that reference a single resource whose id is needed by an ownership-based policy (e.g. ReservationId for Policies.SelfOrAdmin on CancelReservationCommand). The policy enforcer uses this id to look up the resource and resolve its owner — it does not need to know anything else about the request shape.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IReadModel
member.summary: Generic abstraction for a application read model.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IRequestCachingService
member.summary: ⚠️ Warning:. DO NOT USE WHEN USING EVENT SOURCING, AS IT CAN CAUSE INCONSISTENCIES. This service is intended to be used in the command and query pipelines, but not when using projections in an event bus pipeline. Implements the request caching service that handles caching logic for requests implementing the ICacheRequest interface.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IRequestInvalidateCachingService
member.summary: ⚠️ Warning:. DO NOT USE WHEN USING EVENT SOURCING, AS IT CAN CAUSE INCONSISTENCIES. This service is intended to be used in the command and query pipelines, but not when using projections in an event bus pipeline. Implements the request invalidate caching service that handles cache invalidation logic for requests implementing the IInvalidateCacheRequest interface.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ISanitizableRequest
member.summary: Defines a contract for requests that contain sensitive information and require secure representation for logging or traceability purposes. Implement this interface for commands or queries that need to hide, anonymize, or transform part of their data before logging it.

member.name: M:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ISanitizableRequest.GetSanitized
member.summary: Returns a secure representation of the current object, removing or anonymizing sensitive information for logging purposes.
member.returns: An object that securely represents the current request, with its sensitive fields processed.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.IScopedReadModel
member.summary: Marks a read model as scope-aware. All read models implement this so that the tenant query filter and interceptor can operate generically. ScopeInfo is an owned EF Core type flattened into the same table.

member.name: T:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ScopeInfo
member.summary: Flat scope representation for read models. Uses primitive Guids (not domain value objects) to stay decoupled from domain types.

member.name: M:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ScopeInfo.From(JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ScopeContext)
member.summary: Creates a ScopeInfo from the application-layer ScopeContext, mapping DomainId to the same concept without domain type dependency.

member.name: M:JordiAragonZaragoza.SharedKernel.Application.Contracts.Interfaces.ScopeInfo.Matches(System.Guid,System.Nullable{System.Guid},System.Nullable{System.Guid})
member.summary: Mirrors User.Domain.Scope.Matches semantics: the narrowest defined level wins. A read model with no PartitionId/DomainId is visible across the entire tenant.
member.returns: True if the scope matches, false otherwise.
